what_it_does: Records an image attachment in the channel-memory ledger, linking a design-source channel entry to the image asset at image://design-source/capital-raise/p-07.
observations: YAML frontmatter metadata block with no body content
id is att-fcf3aea2 and channel is design-source
standouts: target image URI points to a capital-raise page/slide labeled p-07
file is purely metadata with no descriptive or symbolic content
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
image:// URI scheme for asset references
concerns: file contains no description, caption, or alt text for the image
provenance and author are not recorded
target image asset is not included or verified in the file
notes: A bare metadata stub that attaches a capital-raise image asset to the design-source channel memory.
questions: What visual content does p-07 in capital-raise represent?
Who added this attachment and for what decision or artifact?

purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it is an attachment record and it records an attachment, though it carries almost no descriptive payload.
apparent_themes: image attachments for design and fundraising artifacts
intention_signals: To catalog and make retrievable a specific image asset within the design-source channel, likely as part of a capital-raise document or presentation.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI to reference a sequenced page-like asset (p-07) inside a capital-raise path.
contribution: Provides a machine-readable attachment record that maps a channel memory entry to a specific image asset, supporting retrieval, provenance, and context assembly without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: Markdown attachment metadata file att-fcf3aea2 in the design-source channel records an images attachment pointing to image://design-source/capital-raise/p-07 with an added timestamp and no body content.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to link a capital-raise image asset to the design-source channel memory for later reference and retrieval.
